2604.11632.title: CArtBench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Chinese Art Understanding, Interpretation, and Authenticity
2604.11632.paper_summary: We introduce CARTBENCH, a museum-grounded benchmark for evaluating vision-language models (VLMs) on Chinese artworks beyond short-form recognition and QA. CARTBENCH comprises four subtasks: CURATORQA for evidence-grounded recognition and reasoning, CATALOGCAPTION for structured four-section expert-style appreciation, REINTERPRET for defensible reinterpretation with expert ratings, and CONNOISSEURPAIRS for diagnostic authenticity discrimination under visually similar confounds. CARTBENCH is built by aligning image-bearing Palace Museum objects from Wikidata with authoritative catalog pages, spanning five art categories across multiple dynasties. Across nine representative VLMs, we find that high overall CURATORQA accuracy can mask sharp drops on hard evidence linking and style-to-period inference; long-form appreciation remains far from expert references; and authenticity-oriented diagnostic discrimination stays near chance, underscoring the difficulty of connoisseur-level reasoning for current models.
2604.11632.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹CARTBENCH，一個基於博物館的基準，用於評估視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在中國藝術品上的表現，超越短期識別和問答。CARTBENCH包含四個子任務：CURATORQA用於基於證據的識別和推理，CATALOGCAPTION用於結構化的四部分專家風格欣賞，REINTERPRET用於具有專家評級的可辯護重解，以及CONNOISSEURPAIRS用於在視覺上相似的干擾下進行診斷真實性區分。CARTBENCH通過將來自Wikidata的帶圖像的故宮博物院物品與權威的目錄頁面對齊而構建，涵蓋五個藝術類別，跨越多個朝代。在九個具有代表性的VLM中，我們發現高整體CURATORQA準確率可能掩蓋了在困難證據鏈接和風格到時期推斷上的急劇下降；長期欣賞仍然遠未達到專家參考水平；而以真實性為導向的診斷區分接近隨機，突顯了當前模型在行家級推理上的困難。
2604.11632.author: Xuefeng Wei et.al.
2604.11632.authors: Xuefeng Wei, Zhixuan Wang, Xuan Zhou, Zhi Qu, Hongyao Li, Yusuke Sakai, Hidetaka Kamigaito, Taro Watanabe
